channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: Embracing Anisotropy: Turning Massive Activations into Interpretable Control Knobs for Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.00029v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) exhibit highly anisotropic internal representations, often characterized by massive activations, a phenomenon where a small subset of feature dimensions possesses magnitudes significantly larger than the rest. While prior ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, LLM, arxiv, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Autorubric: A Unified Framework for Rubric-Based LLM Evaluation
item.description: arXiv:2603.00077v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Rubric-based evaluation with large language models (LLMs) has become standard practice for assessing text generation at scale, yet the underlying techniques are scattered across papers with inconsistent terminology and partial solutions. We present a ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> few-shot, paper, model, arxiv, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: DIG to Heal: Scaling General-purpose Agent Collaboration via Explainable Dynamic Decision Paths
item.description: arXiv:2603.00309v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The increasingly popular agentic AI paradigm promises to harness the power of multiple, general-purpose large language model (LLM) agents to collaboratively complete complex tasks. While many agentic AI systems utilize predefined workflows or agent ro...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, large language model, LLM, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: EmCoop: A Framework and Benchmark for Embodied Cooperation Among LLM Agents
item.description: arXiv:2603.00349v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Real-world scenarios increasingly require multiple embodied agents to collaborate in dynamic environments under embodied constraints, as many tasks exceed the capabilities of any single agent. Recent advances in large language models (LLMs) enable hig...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, paper, analysis, model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Transit Network Design with Two-Level Demand Uncertainties: A Machine Learning and Contextual Stochastic Optimization Framework
item.description: arXiv:2603.00010v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Transit Network Design is a well-studied problem in the field of transportation, typically addressed by solving optimization models under fixed demand assumptions. Considering the limitations of these assumptions, this paper proposes a new framework, ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, context, model, arxiv, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Attn-QAT: 4-Bit Attention With Quantization-Aware Training
item.description: arXiv:2603.00040v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Achieving reliable 4-bit attention is a prerequisite for end-to-end FP4 computation on emerging FP4-capable GPUs, yet attention remains the main obstacle due to FP4's tiny dynamic range and attention's heavy-tailed activations. This paper presents the...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, model, arxiv, attention, study | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Econometric vs. Causal Structure-Learning for Time-Series Policy Decisions: Evidence from the UK COVID-19 Policies
item.description: arXiv:2603.00041v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Causal machine learning (ML) recovers graphical structures that inform us about potential cause-and-effect relationships. Most progress has focused on cross-sectional data with no explicit time order, whereas recovering causal structures from time ser...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> example, model, arxiv, research, attention | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Reinforcement Learning for Control with Probabilistic Stability Guarantee: A Finite-Sample Approach
item.description: arXiv:2603.00043v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This paper presents a novel approach to reinforcement learning (RL) for control systems that provides probabilistic stability guarantees using finite data. Leveraging Lyapunov's method, we propose a probabilistic stability theorem that ensures mean sq...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, paper, model, arxiv, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Property-Driven Evaluation of GNN Expressiveness at Scale: Datasets, Framework, and Study
item.description: arXiv:2603.00044v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Advancing trustworthy AI requires principled software engineering approaches to model evaluation. Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) have achieved remarkable success in processing graph-structured data, however, their expressiveness in capturing fundamental...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> embedding, model, arxiv, framework, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: REMIND: Rethinking Medical High-Modality Learning under Missingness--A Long-Tailed Distribution Perspective
item.description: arXiv:2603.00046v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Medical multi-modal learning is critical for integrating information from a large set of diverse modalities. However, when leveraging a high number of modalities in real clinical applications, it is often impractical to obtain full-modality observatio...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, analysis, arxiv, multimodal, framework | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: SimpleTool: Parallel Decoding for Real-Time LLM Function Calling
item.description: arXiv:2603.00030v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: LLM-based function calling enables intelligent agents to interact with external tools and environments, yet autoregressive decoding imposes a fundamental latency bottleneck that limits real-time applications such as embodied intelligence, game AI, and...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, model, arxiv, LLM, tool | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Monotropic Artificial Intelligence: Toward a Cognitive Taxonomy of Domain-Specialized Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.00350v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The prevailing paradigm in artificial intelligence research equates progress with scale: larger models trained on broader datasets are presumed to yield superior capabilities. This assumption, while empirically productive for general-purpose applicati...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> analysis, model, arxiv, framework, research | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Conservative Equilibrium Discovery in Offline Game-Theoretic Multiagent Reinforcement Learning
item.description: arXiv:2603.00374v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Offline learning of strategies takes data efficiency to its extreme by restricting algorithms to a fixed dataset of state-action trajectories. We consider the problem in a mixed-motive multiagent setting, where the goal is to solve a game under the of...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> arxiv, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: StaTS: Spectral Trajectory Schedule Learning for Adaptive Time Series Forecasting with Frequency Guided Denoiser
item.description: arXiv:2603.00037v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Diffusion models have been used for probabilistic time series forecasting and show strong potential. However, fixed noise schedules often produce intermediate states that are hard to invert and a terminal state that deviates from the near noise assump...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, arxiv, experiment | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
